rag_rules: When repairing AGREE contracts, ensure assumptions and guarantees are placed in valid component type scopes and avoid referencing implementation-level bindings or subcomponent ports in type contracts.
If a validator reports a type error due to an unresolved identifier, trace and fix the root cause by ensuring all referenced features or helpers are visible or properly declared in the same annex.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible identifiers and declared helpers; avoid introducing new architecture elements during repair.
Assumptions and guarantees should be scoped to component types unless they depend on implementation-specific details like subcomponent ports or bindings.
When a guarantee references a value or feature, ensure the referenced identifier is either a visible AADL feature or a helper declared within the same annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE expressions; do not reference identifiers from outside the current annex scope.
Place component type contracts in the appropriate component type scope, not in implementations or subcomponent contexts.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings or subcomponent ports in component type contracts, as they are not valid in type scopes.
Do not attempt to resolve type errors by adding new architecture elements; instead, fix or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
AGREE validator diagnostics (none present in this case)
